overall_summary: The "Google – AI Assistants for Data Tasks with Gemma" competition focused on developing specialized AI agents for data science and programming tasks using Google's Gemma model. Winning approaches primarily leveraged parameter-efficient fine-tuning (QLoRA/LoRA) combined with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and ReAct prompting, supported by carefully curated datasets and heuristic prompt engineering to enhance reliability and domain-specific performance.
competition_flows: Not applicable; the text is a curated bibliography of reference kernels rather than a single competition pipeline description.
notable_individual_insights: N/A (Google – AI Assistants for Data Tasks with Gemma): Combining LoRA/QLoRA with RAG and ReAct prompting effectively creates specialized AI assistants for domain-specific tasks.

N/A (Google – AI Assistants for Data Tasks with Gemma): Providing a representative sample of prompt/output pairs for evaluation significantly improves the structure, style, and reliability of RAG outputs.
N/A (Google – AI Assistants for Data Tasks with Gemma): Carefully curated datasets are essential for building robust and accurate LLM-based assistants.
